full_response:
Let the table slots be numbered 1,2,…,m and focus on slot 1.

1.  Probability that slot 1 is set to 1  

   For every call INSERT(w) we perform k independent hash trials  
   h1(w),…,hk(w).  In total, n words ⇒ kn independent trials.

   • For any single trial the probability that it hits slot 1 is 1/m  
     (because h i (w) is uniform on {1,…,m}).  
   • Hence the probability that a given trial misses slot 1 is 1−1/m.  
   • The trials are independent, so the probability that all kn of them
     miss slot 1 is (1−1/m)^{kn}.  

   Therefore  

        P[ slot 1 is set to 1 after all inserts ]
      = 1 − (1−1/m)^{kn}.                    (□)

2.  A lower bound on k that guarantees this probability is ≥ 1/2  

   Require                                      (by (□))
        1 − (1−1/m)^{kn} ≥ 1/2
   ⇔    (1−1/m)^{kn} ≤ 1/2.

   Use the elementary inequality 1−x ≤ e^{−x} (valid for all x):  

        (1−1/m)^{kn} ≤ e^{−kn/m}.

   Thus it suffices to make e^{−kn/m} ≤ 1/2, i.e.  

        −kn/m ≤ ln(1/2) = −ln 2
        kn/m ≥ ln 2
        k ≥ (m ln 2)/n.                       (since k is an integer, take ⌈·⌉)

   Because ln 2 ≈ 0.693, a simple numeric form is  

        k ≥ 0.693 · m / n.

\boxed{\,P[\text{slot 1 is 1}] = 1-(1-\tfrac1m)^{kn}\quad\text{and}\quad
       k\;\ge\;\lceil (m\ln 2)/n\rceil\; \Rightarrow\;P\ge \tfrac12\,}

Confidence: 0.21
